title: How Does Sleep Connect Me to the World?
subtitle: Journal prompts for examining sleep as participation in something larger than one person's routine
excerpt: Five journal prompts from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The prompts examine the relationship between sleep and connection: what rest gives back to the people and life around you, and what you lose when you withhold it. The aim is to move sleep out of the column of things you do for yourself and into the column of things you do as part of a larger whole.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 27 reflection theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

content.text: Five journal prompts from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The prompts examine what sleep actually gives back: to the people around you, to the quality of the day ahead, to the version of you that shows up in the world. Set twenty minutes. Write without stopping.
New to journalling as practice? Read
Who is the version of me that shows up after a good night's sleep?
Not the general idea of 'rested', name specific things. The patience you have, the quality of decisions you make, how you speak to the people around you. Who is that person?
Who is the version of me that shows up after a poor one?
Again, specific. Not 'tired' but the actual behaviours, the shortened reactions, the things you regret later. Write it plainly.
Name one person in your life who benefits when you sleep well.
How specifically does your rest change what they get from you? Write what is different for them when you've slept versus when you haven't.
If sleep were an act of generosity rather than a personal need, what would that change about how you approach it?
Write the specific behaviours, choices, or permissions that would change if you thought of sleep as something you do for others as much as for yourself.
What would you say to someone you loved who was chronically under-sleeping out of guilt?
Write what you'd actually say. Then read it back and note where you're holding yourself to a standard you wouldn't hold them to.
Adapted from the Day 27 reflection theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
